natural_language_question: What are my YTD sales vs budget by store and category?
expected_sql:
WITH sales_ytd AS (
  SELECT
    s.store_id,
    p.category_name,
    SUM(s.revenue_amount) AS actual_sales
  FROM sales_fact s
  JOIN product_dim p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE s.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND s.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
  GROUP BY s.store_id, p.category_name
),
budget_ytd AS (
  SELECT
    store_id,
    category_name,
    SUM(budget_amount) AS budgeted_sales
  FROM budget_plan
  WHERE budget_year = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE())
    AND budget_period = 'YTD'
  GROUP BY store_id, category_name
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(s.store_id, b.store_id) AS store_id,
  COALESCE(s.category_name, b.category_name) AS category_name,
  COALESCE(s.actual_sales, 0) AS actual_sales,
  COALESCE(b.budgeted_sales, 0) AS budgeted_sales,
  s.actual_sales - b.budgeted_sales AS variance,
  CASE
    WHEN b.budgeted_sales = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(100.0 * (s.actual_sales - b.budgeted_sales) / b.budgeted_sales, 2)
  END AS variance_pct
FROM sales_ytd s
FULL OUTER JOIN budget_ytd b ON s.store_id = b.store_id AND s.category_name = b.category_name
ORDER BY variance_pct ASC NULLS LAST, actual_sales DESC;
paraphrases: Show YTD sales compared to budget broken down by store and category
YTD actual sales versus budget by store and category please
How are we performing YTD against budget per store and per category?

natural_language_question: Show gross profit percent MTD versus same month last year by department.
expected_sql:
WITH mtd_current AS (
  SELECT
    p.department_name,
    SUM(s.revenue_amount) AS revenue_mtd,
    SUM(s.cogs_amount) AS cogs_mtd,
    SUM(s.revenue_amount - s.cogs_amount) AS gross_profit_mtd
  FROM sales_fact s
  JOIN product_dim p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE s.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND s.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 month'
  GROUP BY p.department_name
),
mtd_last_year AS (
  SELECT
    p.department_name,
    SUM(s.revenue_amount) AS revenue_ly,
    SUM(s.cogs_amount) AS cogs_ly,
    SUM(s.revenue_amount - s.cogs_amount) AS gross_profit_ly
  FROM sales_fact s
  JOIN product_dim p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE s.transaction_date >= DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND s.transaction_date < DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 month')
  GROUP BY p.department_name
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(c.department_name, ly.department_name) AS department_name,
  c.revenue_mtd,
  c.gross_profit_mtd,
  CASE
    WHEN c.revenue_mtd = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(100.0 * c.gross_profit_mtd / c.revenue_mtd, 2)
  END AS gross_margin_pct_mtd,
  ly.gross_profit_ly,
  CASE
    WHEN ly.revenue_ly = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(100.0 * ly.gross_profit_ly / ly.revenue_ly, 2)
  END AS gross_margin_pct_ly,
  ROUND(
    (CASE WHEN c.revenue_mtd = 0 THEN NULL ELSE 100.0 * c.gross_profit_mtd / c.revenue_mtd END) -
    (CASE WHEN ly.revenue_ly = 0 THEN NULL ELSE 100.0 * ly.gross_profit_ly / ly.revenue_ly END),
    2
  ) AS margin_pct_change
FROM mtd_current c
FULL OUTER JOIN mtd_last_year ly ON c.department_name = ly.department_name
ORDER BY gross_margin_pct_mtd DESC NULLS LAST;
paraphrases: Gross margin MTD vs same month last year by department
MTD gross profit percentage compared to last year same month, by dept
How is gross profit % this month versus last year by department?

natural_language_question: Top 10 slow-moving items this week by store â€” include days on hand and current stock.
paraphrases: What are the 10 slowest moving products this week per store with DOH and stock level
Top 10 slow sellers this week by store including days in inventory and on-hand quantity
Show the slowest moving items right now this week, broken down by store
expected_sql:
SELECT
  s.store_id,
  p.product_id,
  p.product_name,
  SUM(s.units) AS units_sold_week,
  i.on_hand_quantity,
  CASE
    WHEN SUM(s.units) > 0 THEN ROUND(i.on_hand_quantity / SUM(s.units), 1)
    ELSE NULL
  END AS days_on_hand,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.store_id ORDER BY SUM(s.units) ASC) AS rank_by_store
FROM sales_fact s
JOIN product_dim p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN inventory_snapshot i ON s.product_id = i.product_id AND s.store_id = i.store_id
WHERE s.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE())
  AND s.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 week'
GROUP BY s.store_id, p.product_id, p.product_name, i.on_hand_quantity
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.store_id ORDER BY SUM(s.units) ASC) <= 10
ORDER BY s.store_id, rank_by_store;

natural_language_question: Which stores are more than 15% below YTD sales target? Show variance amount too.
paraphrases: Stores more than 15% under YTD sales target with dollar variance
List stores over 15% behind YTD budget and show the gap in $
Which locations are significantly below YTD target (more than 15%)
expected_sql:
WITH ytd_sales AS (
  SELECT
    s.store_id,
    SUM(s.revenue_amount) AS actual_ytd_sales
  FROM sales_fact s
  WHERE s.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND s.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
  GROUP BY s.store_id
),
ytd_targets AS (
  SELECT
    store_id,
    SUM(target_amount) AS ytd_target
  FROM sales_target
  WHERE target_year = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE())
  GROUP BY store_id
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(s.store_id, t.store_id) AS store_id,
  s.actual_ytd_sales,
  t.ytd_target,
  ROUND(s.actual_ytd_sales - t.ytd_target, 2) AS variance_amount,
  ROUND(100.0 * (s.actual_ytd_sales - t.ytd_target) / NULLIF(t.ytd_target, 0), 2) AS variance_pct
FROM ytd_sales s
FULL OUTER JOIN ytd_targets t ON s.store_id = t.store_id
WHERE ROUND(100.0 * (s.actual_ytd_sales - t.ytd_target) / NULLIF(t.ytd_target, 0), 2) < -15
ORDER BY variance_pct ASC;

natural_language_question: Weekly sales trend for Electronics category over the last 12 weeks â€” show as line-ready data.
expected_sql:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('week', s.transaction_date) AS week_start,
  SUM(s.revenue_amount) AS weekly_sales,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.transaction_id) AS transaction_count,
  AVG(s.revenue_amount / NULLIF(s.units, 0)) AS avg_price_per_unit
FROM sales_fact s
JOIN product_dim p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
WHERE p.category_name = 'Electronics'
  AND s.transaction_date >= DATEADD(week, -12, DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE()))
  AND s.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 week'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', s.transaction_date)
ORDER BY week_start ASC;
paraphrases: Electronics weekly sales trend for the last 12 weeks
Show weekly sales of Electronics going back 12 weeks for a line chart
12-week trend of Electronics sales broken by week

natural_language_question: MTD revenue and COGS by channel â€” Retail, Online, Wholesale.
paraphrases: Month-to-date revenue and cost of goods by sales channel
MTD revenue + COGS split by Retail, Online, Wholesale
Current month rev and COGS per channel
expected_sql:
SELECT
  s.channel,
  SUM(s.revenue_amount) AS mtd_revenue,
  SUM(s.cogs_amount) AS mtd_cogs,
  ROUND(SUM(s.revenue_amount) - SUM(s.cogs_amount), 2) AS mtd_gross_profit,
  CASE
    WHEN SUM(s.revenue_amount) = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(100.0 * (SUM(s.revenue_amount) - SUM(s.cogs_amount)) / SUM(s.revenue_amount), 2)
  END AS gross_margin_pct
FROM sales_fact s
WHERE s.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE())
  AND s.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 month'
  AND s.channel IN ('Retail', 'Online', 'Wholesale')
GROUP BY s.channel
ORDER BY mtd_revenue DESC;

natural_language_question: Bottom 5 stores by sales per square meter this quarter.
paraphrases: Worst 5 stores for sales per square foot this quarter
Bottom performing stores by sales/sqm QTD
Which stores have the lowest sales per sqm this quarter
expected_sql:
SELECT
  s.store_id,
  st.store_name,
  st.square_meters,
  SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS qtd_sales,
  ROUND(SUM(sf.revenue_amount) / NULLIF(st.square_meters, 0), 2) AS sales_per_sqm,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(sf.revenue_amount) / NULLIF(st.square_meters, 0) ASC) AS rank
FROM sales_fact sf
JOIN store_dim s ON sf.store_id = s.store_id
JOIN store_details st ON s.store_id = st.store_id
WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE())
  AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 quarter'
GROUP BY s.store_id, st.store_name, st.square_meters
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(sf.revenue_amount) / NULLIF(st.square_meters, 0) ASC) <= 5
ORDER BY sales_per_sqm ASC;

natural_language_question: Year-over-year growth rate for top 20 products by revenue last month.
paraphrases: YoY growth % for the top 20 products by revenue last month
Top 20 SKUs by last month's revenue with year-over-year growth
Which products grew the most YoY among the highest revenue items last month
expected_sql:
WITH last_month_sales AS (
  SELECT
    p.product_id,
    p.product_name,
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS last_month_revenue,
    SUM(sf.units) AS last_month_units
  FROM sales_fact sf
  JOIN product_dim p ON sf.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATEADD(month, -1, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE())
  GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
),
last_month_ly AS (
  SELECT
    p.product_id,
    p.product_name,
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS ly_revenue,
    SUM(sf.units) AS ly_units
  FROM sales_fact sf
  JOIN product_dim p ON sf.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATEADD(month, -13, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATEADD(month, -12, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()))
  GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
),
top_products AS (
  SELECT
    lm.product_id,
    lm.product_name,
    lm.last_month_revenue,
    ly.ly_revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * (lm.last_month_revenue - ly.ly_revenue) / NULLIF(ly.ly_revenue, 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY lm.last_month_revenue DESC) AS rank
  FROM last_month_sales lm
  LEFT JOIN last_month_ly ly ON lm.product_id = ly.product_id
)
SELECT
  product_id,
  product_name,
  last_month_revenue,
  ly_revenue,
  yoy_growth_pct
FROM top_products
WHERE rank <= 20
ORDER BY last_month_revenue DESC;

natural_language_question: Show average transaction value by day of week for the last 90 days.
paraphrases: Average basket size by day of the week over the past 90 days
Last 90 days avg transaction value per weekday
How much customers spend on average each day of week recently
expected_sql:
SELECT
  DAYNAME(sf.transaction_date) AS day_of_week,
  DAYOFWEEK(sf.transaction_date) AS day_num,
  COUNT(DISTINCT sf.transaction_id) AS transaction_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT sf.customer_id) AS customer_count,
  SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS total_revenue,
  ROUND(SUM(sf.revenue_amount) / COUNT(DISTINCT sf.transaction_id), 2) AS avg_transaction_value,
  ROUND(SUM(sf.revenue_amount) / COUNT(DISTINCT sf.customer_id), 2) AS avg_customer_spend
FROM sales_fact sf
WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_DATE())
  AND sf.transaction_date < CURRENT_DATE()
GROUP BY DAYNAME(sf.transaction_date), DAYOFWEEK(sf.transaction_date)
ORDER BY day_num ASC;

natural_language_question: Which categories have the highest return rate YTD? Include return value and % of sales.
paraphrases: Categories with the worst return rates year to date â€” show $ and %
Top categories by return percentage YTD including return amount
Which product categories see the most returns this year
expected_sql:
WITH category_sales AS (
  SELECT
    p.category_name,
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS total_sales,
    SUM(sf.units) AS total_units
  FROM sales_fact sf
  JOIN product_dim p ON sf.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
    AND sf.transaction_type = 'SALE'
  GROUP BY p.category_name
),
category_returns AS (
  SELECT
    p.category_name,
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS return_value,
    SUM(sf.units) AS return_units,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sf.transaction_id) AS return_count
  FROM sales_fact sf
  JOIN product_dim p ON sf.product_id = p.product_id
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
    AND sf.transaction_type = 'RETURN'
  GROUP BY p.category_name
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(s.category_name, r.category_name) AS category_name,
  s.total_sales,
  r.return_value,
  ROUND(100.0 * r.return_value / NULLIF(s.total_sales, 0), 2) AS return_rate_pct,
  r.return_count
FROM category_sales s
FULL OUTER JOIN category_returns r ON s.category_name = r.category_name
ORDER BY return_rate_pct DESC NULLS LAST;

natural_language_question: Monthly operating expenses trend last 12 months with % change.
paraphrases: OpEx by month past year showing month-over-month % change
12-month operating expense trend with growth rates
Monthly OpEx run-rate and % variance last 12 months
expected_sql:
WITH monthly_opex AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', expense_date) AS expense_month,
    SUM(expense_amount) AS monthly_opex
  FROM operating_expenses
  WHERE expense_date >= DATEADD(month, -12, DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND expense_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 month'
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', expense_date)
)
SELECT
  expense_month,
  monthly_opex,
  LAG(monthly_opex) OVER (ORDER BY expense_month) AS prior_month_opex,
  ROUND(monthly_opex - LAG(monthly_opex) OVER (ORDER BY expense_month), 2) AS mom_change_amount,
  CASE
    WHEN LAG(monthly_opex) OVER (ORDER BY expense_month) = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(100.0 * (monthly_opex - LAG(monthly_opex) OVER (ORDER BY expense_month)) / LAG(monthly_opex) OVER (ORDER BY expense_month), 2)
  END AS mom_change_pct
FROM monthly_opex
ORDER BY expense_month ASC;

natural_language_question: Gross profit bridge â€” show volume, mix, cost, price impact YTD vs LY.
paraphrases: YTD gross profit walk â€” volume, mix, price, cost effects vs last year
Gross margin bridge analysis YTD vs prior year
Break down gross profit change into volume mix price cost
expected_sql:
WITH ytd_current AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS revenue_ytd,
    SUM(sf.cogs_amount) AS cogs_ytd,
    SUM(sf.units) AS units_ytd,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sf.product_id) AS product_count
  FROM sales_fact sf
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
),
ytd_ly AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS revenue_ly,
    SUM(sf.cogs_amount) AS cogs_ly,
    SUM(sf.units) AS units_ly,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sf.product_id) AS product_count_ly
  FROM sales_fact sf
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year')
)
SELECT
  c.revenue_ytd,
  ly.revenue_ly,
  ROUND(c.revenue_ytd - ly.revenue_ly, 2) AS revenue_variance,
  ROUND(100.0 * (c.revenue_ytd - ly.revenue_ly) / NULLIF(ly.revenue_ly, 0), 2) AS revenue_variance_pct,
  c.cogs_ytd,
  ly.cogs_ly,
  ROUND(c.cogs_ytd - ly.cogs_ly, 2) AS cogs_variance,
  ROUND((c.revenue_ytd - c.cogs_ytd) - (ly.revenue_ly - ly.cogs_ly), 2) AS gp_variance,
  ROUND(100.0 * ((c.revenue_ytd - c.cogs_ytd) - (ly.revenue_ly - ly.cogs_ly)) / NULLIF(ly.revenue_ly - ly.cogs_ly, 0), 2) AS gp_variance_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * (c.revenue_ytd - c.cogs_ytd) / NULLIF(c.revenue_ytd, 0), 2) AS gp_margin_pct_ytd,
  ROUND(100.0 * (ly.revenue_ly - ly.cogs_ly) / NULLIF(ly.revenue_ly, 0), 2) AS gp_margin_pct_ly
FROM ytd_current c
CROSS JOIN ytd_ly ly;

natural_language_question: Full P&L summary YTD â€” revenue, COGS, gross profit, op ex, EBITDA, net income â€” with % variance to budget and LY.
paraphrases: Year-to-date full profit & loss with budget and prior year variances
YTD P&L key lines â€” actual vs budget vs last year
Complete YTD income statement with % variances
expected_sql:
WITH ytd_pl AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(sf.cogs_amount) AS cogs,
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount - sf.cogs_amount) AS gross_profit,
    SUM(oe.expense_amount) FILTER (WHERE oe.expense_category = 'OPERATING') AS operating_expenses,
    SUM(oe.expense_amount) FILTER (WHERE oe.expense_category = 'INTEREST') AS interest_expense,
    SUM(oe.expense_amount) FILTER (WHERE oe.expense_category = 'TAX') AS tax_expense
  FROM sales_fact sf
  LEFT JOIN operating_expenses oe ON sf.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
    AND oe.expense_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND oe.expense_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year'
),
ytd_ly_pl AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount) AS revenue_ly,
    SUM(sf.cogs_amount) AS cogs_ly,
    SUM(sf.revenue_amount - sf.cogs_amount) AS gross_profit_ly,
    SUM(oe.expense_amount) FILTER (WHERE oe.expense_category = 'OPERATING') AS operating_expenses_ly
  FROM sales_fact sf
  LEFT JOIN operating_expenses oe ON oe.expense_date >= DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND oe.expense_date < DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year')
  WHERE sf.transaction_date >= DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()))
    AND sf.transaction_date < DATEADD(year, -1, DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE()) + INTERVAL '1 year')
)
SELECT
  p.revenue,
  p.cogs,
  p.gross_profit,
  ROUND(100.0 * p.gross_profit / NULLIF(p.revenue, 0), 2) AS gross_margin_pct,
  p.operating_expenses,
  ROUND(p.gross_profit - p.operating_expenses, 2) AS ebitda,
  p.interest_expense,
  p.tax_expense,
  ROUND(p.gross_profit - p.operating_expenses - p.interest_expense - p.tax_expense, 2) AS net_income,
  ROUND(100.0 * (p.gross_profit - p.operating_expenses - p.interest_expense - p.tax_expense) / NULLIF(p.revenue, 0), 2) AS net_margin_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * (p.revenue - ly.revenue_ly) / NULLIF(ly.revenue_ly, 0), 2) AS revenue_variance_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * (p.gross_profit - ly.gross_profit_ly) / NULLIF(ly.gross_profit_ly, 0), 2) AS gp_variance_pct
FROM ytd_pl p
CROSS JOIN ytd_ly_pl ly;

natural_language_question: Compare online vs in-store sales mix MTD vs last year same period.
expected_features: MTD vs same month last year
paraphrases: MTD online vs in-store sales split compared to last year same month

natural_language_question: Stores ranked by shrink percentage YTD â€” show top 10 worst.

paraphrases: Foot traffic and conversion trend week by week past 8 weeks

natural_language_question: Products with negative margin last month â€” include cost and selling price.
paraphrases: Items sold at a loss last month with cost and price details

natural_language_question: Sales per labor hour by store for the current payroll week.

paraphrases: Percentage of online customers who also shopped in physical stores past 90 days

natural_language_question: Forecast vs actual sales for Black Friday week last year vs this year.
paraphrases: Black Friday week actual vs forecast this year and last year
